positive.prompt: Grill me on this architecture plan and record the clarified terminology and major decisions as we go
Stress-test this design by asking me dependency-aware questions before anyone implements it
Interrogate this proposal using terminal choice prompts and keep the working CONTEXT and ADRs local

negative.prompt: Turn this accepted specification into ordered implementation tasks
Before merging this branch, review the complete diff for correctness and security issues

evals.prompt: Grill me on a proposed multi-tenant billing design. Some questions depend on the tenancy model, while retention and invoice-export preferences are independent. Keep any working documentation local.
evals.expected_output: A bounded standard-mode interview that prioritizes tenancy as a visible P0 decision, shows dynamic progress, records only session-local drafts, and cleans those drafts after a confirmed terminal handoff
expectations: The agent inspects available repository evidence for facts and asks the user only for decisions
The agent classifies candidate nodes as P0, P1, or P2 and asks the tenancy blocker before dependent questions
The agent uses the single standard budget of at most three batches, six decision questions, and three dependency-safe questions per batch; it does not ask the user to choose a fast or deep mode
Independent same-priority sibling decisions may be batched, with no more than three questions in one batch
The agent prefers one native structured-question call per batch and provides a numbered text fallback when unavailable
Every question has concrete alternatives, a recommended answer, and enough context to decide
Before every batch the agent reports actual progress: batch and question budget, P0/P1 state, and pruned or Deferred work
The agent reconciles each answer batch and recomputes the decision graph before continuing
Working documents live only in a unique sessions/<id> directory under the managed workspace; no shared CONTEXT.md or ADR root is treated as disposable
CONTEXT.md contains domain language rather than implementation details, and an ADR is provisional unless separately promoted
The agent asks for confirmation of shared understanding, does not implement or promote documents implicitly, and removes only its completed session drafts

evals.prompt: Grill this multi-tenant billing proposal. Tenant isolation is a P0; invoice-export format and retention each have a recommended P1 default. After the P0 is resolved, I may say 'accept the P1 defaults and close.' Treat docs/plans/billing.md as a protected existing plan: do not edit it unless I explicitly authorize an update, and remove this interview's temporary records after the handoff.
evals.expected_output: A priority-aware interview that can explicitly truncate after P0 resolution, reports the chosen P1 defaults and Deferred items, preserves the protected plan, and cleans only its own session directory
expectations: The assistant never defaults or defers the P0 tenant-isolation decision
If the user explicitly accepts P1 defaults, the terminal outcome is TRUNCATED rather than pretending every preference was independently decided
The final handoff names the accepted P1 defaults, Deferred items, remaining risks, and cleanup result
The protected docs/plans/billing.md path is neither overwritten nor deleted without explicit authorization
Temporary INTERVIEW.md, CONTEXT.md, and provisional ADRs are scoped to the current managed session and are removed only after the handoff

evals.prompt: Use grill-with-docs on this launch plan. If three batches or six decision questions are exhausted while a privacy/data-isolation P0 remains unresolved, stop immediately. Do not ask more questions or manufacture a final plan; retain only the current session record needed to resume or cancel safely.
evals.expected_output: A bounded interview that surfaces a BLOCKED outcome for the unresolved P0, explains the impact, and preserves the owned session state instead of silently exceeding its budget or deleting recovery information
expectations: The assistant enforces the same three-batch and six-decision standard budget even when important uncertainty remains
An unresolved P0 at the limit produces BLOCKED, not a P1 default, a fourth batch, or a fabricated final plan
The final handoff identifies the unresolved P0 and what it blocks
BLOCKED or AWAITING_USER_DECISION interview state is retained for recovery; it is not recursively deleted with the shared workspace
